title: The best Mexico City neighbourhoods to swap into
description: Planning a home swap into CDMX? These are the neighbourhoods that suit a longer stay — walkable, café-rich, and built for remote work and slow travel.
hero: Mexico City is a place you settle into, not rush through. For a home swap of a few weeks or more, these are the neighbourhoods that make it feel like home.

sections.heading: How to think about it
sections.body:
CDMX is vast and varies block to block. For a swap you want walkability, tree-lined streets, good coffee, and an easy hop to the rest of the city.
The areas below are where remote workers and longer-stay travellers actually live — central without being chaotic.

sections.heading: 1. Roma Norte
sections.body: The default for a reason: leafy streets, third-wave cafés, galleries, and dinner anywhere you point. Walkable and social. Best for: first-time CDMX swappers and remote workers.

sections.heading: 2. Condesa
sections.body: Art-deco buildings wrapped around Parque México and Parque España. Calmer and greener than Roma, dog-walkers everywhere. Best for: a relaxed, parks-and-cafés month.

sections.heading: 3. Juárez
sections.body: Between Reforma and Roma — central, fast-changing, with great food and quick access to everything. Best for: people who want to be in the middle of the action.

sections.heading: 4. Coyoacán
sections.body: Cobblestones, the Frida Kahlo house, weekend markets, and a real village feel further south. Quieter and more local. Best for: families and slow travellers who don't need nightlife at the door.

sections.heading: 5. San Miguel Chapultepec
sections.body: Low-key, residential, and next to Chapultepec park and the big museums. Space and calm a short ride from Condesa. Best for: longer stays that want quiet over buzz.

sections.heading: 6. Escandón
sections.body: Less hyped, more affordable, and wedged conveniently between Condesa and the business districts. Increasingly where locals priced out of Roma move. Best for: a real-neighbourhood, value-minded swap.

sections.heading: Working remotely from CDMX
sections.body:
The city runs on café laptops and strong wifi, and the time zone overlaps well with the US and (in the morning) Europe — part of why CDMX ⇄ Lisbon and CDMX ⇄ Brooklyn are early swapl corridors.
Altitude is real (2,240m) — give yourself a couple of slow days when you arrive.

sections.heading: Swapping in or out of CDMX
sections.body:
CDMX is a creative-city corridor on swapl with demand from Brooklyn, Lisbon, Paris and Berlin, where longer stays and flexible dates are the norm.
List your CDMX home before September to become a founding host — shown first when matches open.
